report_name: UK Biobank Breach: Health Data of 500,000 Volunteers Found for Sale on Alibaba

threat_actors.name: Leaking Chinese Research Institutions
threat_actors.description: Three unnamed Chinese research institutions that had legitimate access to UK Biobank data but subsequently leaked it, leading to it being listed for sale on Alibaba's platforms. The incident is described as a data governance failure and a compromise of a trusted partner in the data supply chain.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: The Chinese research institutions were granted legitimate access to the UK Biobank data through a trusted relationship, which was subsequently compromised or mismanaged, leading to the data leak.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: The incident was classified as a supply chain attack where the weak link was a trusted human partner (the research institutions) rather than a software component, leading to the leak of sensitive data.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The leaked data was exfiltrated from the research partners' systems and moved to an unauthorized location, specifically Alibaba's e-commerce platforms where it was listed for sale.

identities.name: UK Biobank
identities.description: A UK-based large-scale biomedical database and research resource, whose de-identified health data from 500,000 volunteers was leaked by third-party research partners.

identities.name: Alibaba
identities.description: Chinese multinational e-commerce company whose platforms were used to list the stolen UK Biobank data for sale.

identities.name: UK Government
identities.description: Confirmed the data breach, collaborated with Chinese authorities to remove the listings, and was informed of the incident by UK Biobank.

identities.name: Chinese Government
identities.description: Collaborated with the UK government to have the data listings removed from Alibaba's platforms.

identities.name: Information Commissioner's Office
identities.description: The UK's data protection regulator to which the UK Biobank self-reported the incident for investigation of potential GDPR violations.

infrastructure.name: Alibaba E-commerce Sales Platform
infrastructure.description: E-commerce platforms owned by Alibaba were used as an unauthorized location to list and offer the leaked UK Biobank dataset for sale.
